title: Crisis / First 72 Hours
summary: For families in immediate chaos who need to slow things down and make safer decisions.
goal: Help families stabilize, stop reacting blindly, and choose their next step with more clarity.
audience: Families dealing with active use, emotional escalation, or immediate fear.

forumTopic.prompt: What feels most urgent in your family right now — and what decision are you afraid to make?

title: Boundary-Setting
summary: For families who know they need change but keep collapsing under pressure.
goal: Teach clear, enforceable boundaries without rage, rescuing, or inconsistency.
audience: Parents, spouses, and siblings who feel trapped in repeating patterns.

forumTopic.prompt: What boundary do you know you need to set — and what keeps making it hard to hold?

title: Treatment Decision
summary: For families trying to figure out what kind of help is actually needed.
goal: Make families smarter consumers so they stop guessing and stop getting sold.
audience: Families comparing detox, inpatient, outpatient, sober living, and next-step options.

resourcePaths.title: 40 Questions to Ask a Treatment Center
Matching the Modality to the Problem
Why Some Therapies Fail at the Wrong Time

forumTopic.prompt: What part of treatment planning feels most confusing right now — level of care, timing, cost, or trust?

title: Relapse Response
summary: For families dealing with return-to-use, denial, or repeated failed attempts.
goal: Shift from panic and disappointment into a more strategic response.
audience: Families who have already tried treatment, promises, or multiple recovery attempts.

resourcePaths.title: Why Multiple Treatment Episodes Don't Mean Failure

forumTopic.prompt: How has your family usually responded after relapse — and what do you want to do differently next time?

title: Family Systems / Enabling
summary: For families stuck in the same painful loop and ready to understand the pattern underneath it.
goal: Show how the family system adapts around addiction and how to stop feeding it.
audience: Families noticing secrecy, rescuing, resentment, role confusion, and chronic chaos.

resourcePaths.title: How Families Accidentally Interfere With Recovery

forumTopic.prompt: What pattern keeps repeating in your family no matter how hard everyone tries?

title: Spouse / Partner
summary: For partners dealing with exhaustion, mistrust, anger, confusion, and grief.
goal: Help spouses regain clarity, dignity, and emotional footing.
audience: Spouses and partners who feel emotionally worn down by addiction.

resourcePaths.title: Is It Safe to Open Up Again?
Grief for the Family You Thought You'd Have

forumTopic.prompt: What has addiction pushed to the edges of your own life — sleep, peace, friendships, work, or trust in yourself?

title: Parent
summary: For parents trying to help without controlling everything.
goal: Move parents from fear-driven rescuing to steadier leadership.
audience: Parents of teens, young adults, or adult children dealing with substance use.

resourcePaths.title: The Hidden Cost of Being the Strong One
Why Change Doesn't Happen When Families Try Harder

forumTopic.prompt: As a parent, where do you feel the pull to rescue the most — money, housing, emotional fallout, or fear of what happens if you stop?

title: Sibling
summary: For siblings who often get ignored in addiction recovery content.
goal: Give siblings language for grief, anger, invisibility, and repair.
audience: Brothers and sisters navigating loyalty conflicts, family role shifts, and long-term resentment.

resourcePaths.title: Sibling Guilt, Anger, and Loyalty Conflicts
Growing Up in the Shadow of Addiction

forumTopic.prompt: What do you wish someone understood about being the sibling in a family shaped by addiction?
